newsSinceJun6: No material fundamental news since June 6. The two big catalysts - record fiscal Q2 FY26 results (revenue $3.62B, above high end of guide) and the $1.5B all-cash acquisition of Empower Semiconductor for AI data-center high-density power - were announced May 19-20 and already drove the +5.1% (Jun 2) / +3.3% (Jun 3) rerating to ~$423. Since June 6 the tape is sell-the-news drift: -1.5% today tracking a ~3% tech-equipment sector down day, plus insider-selling optics (CEO Roche sold 10k shares at $404.25 and SVP Cotter 17,594 shares at $418 in early June under option exercises, ~$14.6M sold over 3 months). No analyst rating changes since Wells Fargo's May 13 $470 target. Flow-driven pullback, thesis intact.

sources.label: TradingKey - ADI down on Jun 9, facts behind the move
ADI press release - Empower Semiconductor $1.5B acquisition (May 19, 2026)
PR Newswire - ADI record fiscal Q2 2026 results
Benzinga - ADI analyst ratings (no moves since May 13)

verdicts.rankRationale: On a residual-alpha list, a $197B mega-cap with Buy consensus, +45% YTD and a post-earnings rerating already in the tape has little hidden alpha left; but the ~6% fade from the June 2 peak with the Empower AI-power thesis intact offsets, so hold at 38 with mild drift down.
verdicts.bull: Empower adds vertical/integrated power delivery for AI accelerators - exactly the power-monitoring and signal-chain central-node thesis - and Q2 beat across all end markets shows the cycle has turned up.
verdicts.neutral: Both catalysts are three weeks old and fully priced; since June 6 the stock has simply drifted down with the sector, leaving rank and score roughly right.
verdicts.bear: 57x trailing P/E, $14.6M of insider selling near the highs, and broad sell-side ownership mean ADI is now a consensus quality name, not an under-owned alpha source.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Empower Semiconductor close in 2H26 and first design-win disclosures in AI data-center power delivery.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding plus valuation: any industrial-cycle wobble or AI-capex digestion compresses a 57x multiple fast.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already pushed ADI down 62->76 on the post-rerating crowding argument; nothing new since June 6 and today's -1.5% is sector-driven, so re-moving would be double-counting.
verdicts.bull: Empower makes ADI more direct to the AI power-delivery node than the legacy 'analog/power/RF' tier tag implies, and Q2 communications strength confirms data-center content growth.
verdicts.neutral: Exposure remains selective and less direct than HBM, advanced packaging or CPO names; rank 76 already reflects that after yesterday's demotion.
verdicts.bear: ADI is still predominantly an industrial/auto analog franchise riding a cyclical recovery; the AI node share of revenue is too small to justify climbing this list.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Evidence that Empower's integrated voltage regulators win sockets next to AI accelerators (power-density bottleneck).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Less direct bottleneck exposure than peers means it de-rates with the sector without the offsetting AI revenue torque.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday already cut it 86->102 on the no-early-CPO-exposure argument; no CPO-relevant news since June 6, so hold at 102 rather than compounding the move.
verdicts.bull: If co-packaged optics drives power-density pain inside the rack, ADI/Empower power delivery becomes an adjacent beneficiary even without direct CPO content.
verdicts.neutral: Quality analog franchise, but the CPO lens is the wrong one for ADI; rank near the bottom of this list is appropriate and unchanged by this week's tape.
verdicts.bear: Zero direct optical/CPO exposure plus industrial-cycle risk at 57x earnings makes ADI dead weight on a CPO alpha list.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Any disclosed power-delivery attach to CPO switch or optical-engine programs.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Industrial cycle rollover with no CPO optionality to cushion the multiple.
